instructions: Do not remove feature entries from this file unless the product scope has genuinely changed.
Treat `passes` as the source of truth for completion.
Only mark `passes` as true after the listed automated and/or manual verification succeeds.
Prefer updating `status`, `notes`, and `lastVerifiedAt` over rewriting titles or steps.

features.title: Headings render in WYSIWYG mode without mutating the markdown source
steps: Load a markdown document that starts with `#` or `##` headings.
Move the caret away from the heading line in WYSIWYG mode.
Confirm the rendered heading is visible while the underlying source text remains unchanged.

manual: Open the editor and move the caret in and out of heading lines.

features.notes: Covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/inlineDecorations.test.tsx`.

features.title: Inline emphasis, inline code, and strikethrough hide syntax away from the caret
steps: Type markdown with bold, italic, inline code, and strikethrough markers.
Move the caret out of each formatted span.
Confirm the rendered text appears without changing the underlying source string.

manual: Check that formatted inline spans still reveal raw markdown when edited directly.

features.notes: Covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/inlineDecorations.test.tsx`.

features.title: Fenced code blocks render safely and preserve fenced markdown source
steps: Open a document containing fenced code blocks.
Move the caret inside and outside the fenced region.
Confirm the code content and fence markers remain intact in the underlying document.

manual: Visually verify that code blocks remain readable in both source and WYSIWYG modes.

features.notes: Covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/codeBlockDecoration.test.ts`; smoke verification on 2026-04-09 also fixed an invalid cross-line replace decoration.

features.title: Switching between WYSIWYG and source modes preserves document text, selection, and undo history
steps: Type into the editor and create unsaved changes.
Toggle from WYSIWYG to source and back.
Confirm document content, selection, and history still reflect the live editing session.

manual: Type into the starter document, toggle modes repeatedly, and confirm no edits disappear.

features.notes: Fixed by preserving a single EditorView and reconfiguring WYSIWYG extensions through a CodeMirror compartment; covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`.

features.title: List markers and blockquotes render contextually in WYSIWYG mode
steps: Open markdown with unordered lists, ordered lists, task lists, nested lists, and nested blockquotes.
Move the caret between those blocks and adjacent plain text.
Confirm syntax markers or checkbox widgets hide when appropriate and remain editable when the caret is inside the block.

manual: Optional visual smoke-check in the app that nested list indentation and blockquote styling match the rendered widgets.

features.notes: Covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/listAndBlockquoteDecoration.test.tsx`, including unordered lists, ordered lists, task lists, blockquotes, nested lists, nested blockquotes, and direct task-checkbox toggling back into markdown source. Nested blockquote rendering was fixed to avoid duplicate line decorations and to hide stacked `>` prefixes when the caret is outside the quote tree.

features.title: Smart list continuation and exit behavior work on Enter
steps: Start an unordered or ordered list in the editor.
Press Enter at the end of a non-empty list item.
Press Enter on an empty list item and confirm the list exits cleanly.

manual: Verify numbering increments correctly for ordered lists.

features.notes: Covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts`.

features.title: Opening a markdown file from the desktop shell hydrates the editor state and active file path
steps: Launch the Electron app with a markdown file path or use File > Open.
Confirm the renderer receives the file content and path.
Verify the editor shows the opened content instead of the starter document.

manual: Open a real `.md` file from Finder or the File menu and verify the buffer updates.

features.notes: Renderer now hydrates from both `file-opened` events and `getCurrentDocument()` for startup recovery; covered by `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`.

features.title: Save and Save As write the live editor buffer rather than the starter template
steps: Open or create a document and make edits in the editor.
Use Save and Save As from the menu or renderer commands.
Confirm the written file contents match the current CodeMirror document.

manual: Save a file, reopen it, and confirm the latest edits persist.

features.notes: Desktop menu actions now route through the renderer so save operations always use the live CodeMirror buffer; covered by `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`.

features.title: New file flow clears document state, dirty state, and represented filename cleanly
steps: Open an existing file in the desktop app.
Trigger File > New.
Confirm the editor resets intentionally, window title changes to `Untitled`, and no stale content remains.

manual: Confirm creating a new file does not overwrite the previously opened file path.

features.notes: Renderer now resets to an empty untitled document on `new-file`, and the Electron main process clears the represented filename; covered by `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`.

features.title: Rendered links are clickable and open externally from the desktop shell
steps: Create markdown with external links, an internal heading link, and at least one reference-style link.
Render the document in WYSIWYG mode and move the caret away from each link.
Confirm `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+click opens external URLs in the system browser, internal links jump within the document, and the Electron window never navigates away.

automated: Add focused coverage for `linkDecoration.ts` link parsing/rendering plus the Electron external-link and internal-anchor routing path.
manual: In the desktop app verify external URLs, internal heading anchors, and a local markdown file link resolve with Typora-style behavior.

features.notes: Typora documents `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+click behavior plus external, internal, local, reference, and auto URL link handling. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Links/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/. Completed on 2026-04-10: MarkFlow now renders inline links, reference-style links, bare URLs, and autolinks as clickable anchors in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/linkDecoration.ts`; `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx` routes Cmd/Ctrl+click internal anchors to matching headings and local markdown links through the desktop bridge; and the Electron shell blocks app-local `window.open` calls while keeping external URLs delegated to the system browser. Verified with targeted editor/desktop Vitest coverage plus full `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm harness:verify`, and `./harness/init.sh --smoke`.

features.title: Images render as inline previews while preserving markdown source
steps: Open markdown containing image syntax that uses both remote URLs and relative/local file paths.
Render the document in WYSIWYG mode.
Confirm the preview is visible and the raw markdown remains editable when focused.
features.notes: Automated coverage now verifies remote URLs, relative local paths resolved against the active markdown document path, absolute filesystem paths preserved, caret-driven editability, broken-image fallback, and App-level file-path plumbing via `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/linkDecoration.test.tsx` and `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`. Typora's broader image workflow still includes drag/drop, paste-from-clipboard, and file copy/upload operations, which remain separate under `MF-029`. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Images/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Theme engine hot-reloads CSS themes and lets the user switch themes
steps: Install or modify a theme stylesheet.
Trigger the theme watcher.
Confirm the editor updates its appearance without a full restart.

automated: Add theme watcher integration tests in the desktop package.
manual: Switch between at least two distinct themes and verify persisted selection.

features.notes: Completed on 2026-04-10. `packages/desktop/src/main/themeManager.ts` now bootstraps built-in CSS themes into the desktop user-data directory, persists the selected theme, and hot-reloads the active stylesheet through chokidar watchers. `packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts`, `packages/shared/src/index.ts`, and `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` expose the theme bridge to the renderer so the titlebar theme selector can switch themes and apply CSS overrides without restarting the app. Verified with focused desktop `themeManager.test.ts`, renderer `App.test.tsx`, plus full `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm harness:verify`, and `./harness/init.sh --smoke` runs.

features.title: Tables render in WYSIWYG mode and stay editable as markdown
steps: Open a markdown table.
Render it in WYSIWYG mode.
Edit cells and confirm the markdown source remains valid.

automated: Add table parsing and editing tests.
manual: Verify column alignment and multiline content behavior.

features.notes: This should wait until core view-state preservation is stable.

features.title: Math blocks render through KaTeX or MathJax without corrupting source markup
steps: Open inline and block math syntax.
Render math in WYSIWYG mode.
Confirm the original markdown or fenced math text remains intact when editing.

manual: Verify invalid expressions fail gracefully.

features.notes: Inline $...$ and block $$...$$ render via KaTeX. Cursor-aware source reveal. Skips code ranges. CSS: .mf-math-inline, .mf-math-block.

features.title: Export pipeline can generate HTML and PDF from the active document
steps: Open a markdown document with headings, lists, code, and links.
Export to HTML and PDF.
Confirm the exported artifacts preserve document structure and styling.

automated: Add snapshot or golden tests for export output.
manual: Open exported artifacts in external viewers and confirm fidelity.

features.notes: Phase 2 feature with strong dependency on stable file IO and rendering.

features.title: Plugins can register markdown post-processors for custom render transforms
steps: Define a plugin registration API.
Load a sample plugin that transforms markdown output.
Confirm the plugin can add a render behavior without breaking core editing.

manual: Install a sample plugin and verify its lifecycle events (optional spot-check).

features.notes: Fully implemented: MarkFlowPluginHost in packages/shared/src/index.ts provides registerMarkdownPostProcessor, load, unload, setPlugins, and runMarkdownPostProcessors. The markdownPostProcessorExtension in packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/markdownPostProcessor.ts hooks the plugin host into CodeMirror via ViewPlugin. A sample plugin (createExternalLinkBadgePlugin in packages/editor/src/plugins/externalLinkBadgePlugin.ts) demonstrates the API by classifying external links. App.tsx wires the plugin host into the editor on startup. Two automated tests cover plugin lifecycle (register/run/unload) and source text preservation.

features.title: Opt-in Yjs collaboration can bootstrap a shared document session
steps: Create a shared document provider.
Join two editor clients to the same document.
Confirm edits converge without source corruption.

manual: Verify presence and reconnect scenarios (requires live WebSocket provider, optional).

features.notes: Implemented as opt-in: yjs and y-codemirror.next added to @markflow/editor deps. packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/yCollab.ts exports yCollabExtension (binds a Y.Doc to CodeMirror via y-codemirror.next) and mergeYDocs (exchange state vectors for test/manual sync). Four CRDT convergence unit tests verify: single-peer sync, concurrent inserts from two offline peers, concurrent delete+insert, and markdown source preservation on no-op sync. Network provider (y-websocket) integration is left as a follow-up; current automated coverage exercises the full CRDT merge path without network.

features.title: Main branch merges produce installable desktop beta builds automatically
steps: Configure CI to build editor and desktop packages on `main`.
Publish beta artifacts for supported platforms.
Confirm the release notes and update channel match the published build.

automated: Validate .github/workflows/ci.yml contains lint, test, build, and release-beta jobs
manual: Install a beta build and confirm auto-update metadata resolves (requires GH_TOKEN and macOS runner).

features.notes: CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml runs lint (pnpm -r run lint), test (pnpm test — all packages), and build (pnpm build — full workspace) on every push and PR to main. The release-beta job runs after all checks pass on main push, builds the Electron app via electron-builder on macOS, and publishes via GH_TOKEN. electron-builder.yml is configured with DMG+ZIP targets for x64 and arm64. Also fixed in this run: TS4094 error in markdownPostProcessor.ts caused by private members on exported anonymous class type — renamed to named class with _ prefix convention.

features.title: Focus mode and typewriter mode keep attention on the active writing block
steps: Open a long markdown document with multiple paragraphs and headings.
Enable focus mode and confirm non-active blocks fade while the active block stays readable.
Enable typewriter mode and confirm the caret remains anchored in a stable viewport position while typing.

automated: Add editor view-state tests for focus styling and stable caret scrolling behavior.
manual: Toggle both modes on a long document and verify they remain usable in both source and WYSIWYG views.

features.notes: Missing from the current ledger despite Phase 2 mentioning focus mode. Typora sources: https://support.typora.io/Focus-and-Typewriter-Mode/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Outline panel shows heading hierarchy and supports quick navigation in long documents
steps: Open a document with nested headings from multiple levels.
Show the outline panel and confirm it mirrors the heading hierarchy.
Click outline entries and verify the editor scrolls to the target section and highlights the active heading.

manual: Optional interactive pass: open a long document and confirm the panel scroll feel remains comfortable after repeated heading edits.

features.notes: Implemented with parser-backed heading derivation in `packages/editor/src/editor/outline.ts`, a right-hand outline panel in `packages/editor/src/App.tsx`, and navigation/highlight coverage in `src/editor/__tests__/outline.test.ts` plus `src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`, including duplicate-anchor handling, fenced-code exclusion, and live rename/reorder sync. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Outline/ and https://support.typora.io/Quick-Start/.

features.title: A `[toc]` block expands into a live table of contents that stays in sync with headings
steps: Insert `[toc]` into a markdown document, press `Return`, and confirm it becomes a rendered table of contents block in the document.
Add, remove, and rename headings across the document.
Confirm the TOC updates automatically, excludes fenced-code pseudo-headings, preserves duplicate-heading anchor targets, and its entries navigate to the correct sections.

automated: Add focused TOC tests for `[toc]` block rendering, parser-backed heading extraction parity with `outline.ts`, duplicate-heading anchor stability, fenced-code exclusion, and modifier-click navigation to generated heading targets.
manual: Open a document containing duplicate headings, setext headings, and a fenced code block with `# fake`; confirm the in-document TOC ignores the fenced line, tracks heading edits live, and its entries jump to the expected section.

features.notes: Now uses extractOutlineHeadings from outline.ts for parser-backed heading extraction. Handles ATX and setext headings, de-duplicates anchors, and excludes fenced-code pseudo-headings. TOC anchor elements use class=mf-link so modifier-click navigation is handled by the editor. Covered by packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/tocDecoration.test.tsx (7 tests).

features.title: Footnote references render cleanly and reveal editable note content without corrupting markdown
steps: Open markdown containing inline footnote references and matching footnote definitions.
Render the document in WYSIWYG mode and confirm references appear as superscripts or equivalent footnote markers.
Hover or otherwise reveal the referenced footnote content, edit the note text, and confirm the underlying markdown references and definitions stay intact.

manual: Optional in-app spot-check: verify multi-paragraph footnotes and long hover-preview text remain readable while the raw `[^label]: ...` definitions stay editable.

features.notes: Implemented with `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/footnoteDecoration.ts`, which renders `[^label]` references as superscript markers, derives hover-preview text from matching `[^label]: ...` definitions, and keeps raw markdown visible whenever the caret enters the reference or definition. `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/linkDecoration.ts` now skips footnote-shaped `Link` nodes so definitions are not misclassified as regular links, and `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/footnoteDecoration.test.tsx` covers caret reveal, repeated references, definition-edit round trips, missing definitions, and code/non-footnote false-positive avoidance. Sources: https://support.typora.io/media/export/Markdown-Reference.pdf and https://typora.io/.

features.title: Mermaid and other diagram fences render live previews while preserving fenced source
steps: Open fenced diagram blocks such as Mermaid flowcharts or sequence diagrams.
Render the document in WYSIWYG mode and confirm a live diagram preview appears outside the active edit state.
Edit the fenced source and verify the preview refreshes without mutating or losing the original markdown block.

manual: Verify large diagrams and invalid Mermaid syntax degrade gracefully without breaking adjacent content.

features.notes: Fenced mermaid blocks render live SVG via Mermaid.js (lazy-loaded). Cursor-aware source reveal. SVG cache. CSS: .mf-mermaid.

features.title: Horizontal rules render as separators in WYSIWYG mode without losing the underlying markdown
steps: Open markdown containing horizontal rules such as `---` or `***`.
Move the caret onto and away from the rule line in WYSIWYG mode.
Confirm a rendered separator appears when unfocused and the original markdown stays editable when focused.

automated: Run `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- listAndBlockquoteDecoration.test.tsx` to cover `---` and `***` horizontal rule rendering, caret focus behavior, and adjacent paragraph integrity.

features.notes: Typora documents horizontal rules as core Markdown syntax, and MarkFlow's existing `HorizontalRule` decoration path in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/blockquoteDecoration.ts` now has direct automated coverage for both `---` and `***`, including the focus/editability transition and adjacent paragraph preservation. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/ and https://support.typora.io/Quick-Start/.

features.title: YAML front matter remains intact and editable as document metadata
steps: Open a markdown document containing YAML front matter delimited by `---`.
Edit both metadata fields and normal body content while switching between source and WYSIWYG mode.
Confirm the front matter block remains intact and no keys, delimiters, or body content are corrupted.

manual: Optional manual spot-check: edit `title`, `tags`, `typora-root-url`, and `typora-copy-images-to` keys while switching modes, and confirm the YAML block remains unchanged.

features.notes: Implemented in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/yamlFrontMatter.ts` and covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/yamlFrontMatter.test.ts`, which verifies top-of-file detection, `---` / `...` fences, and document-preserving decorations. The current verified scope is preservation and visual distinction of YAML metadata in the editor; MarkFlow does not yet consume Typora-specific keys such as `typora-root-url` or `typora-copy-images-to` for export or image workflows. Sources: https://support.typora.io/YAML/ and https://support.typora.io/media/export/Markdown-Reference.pdf.

features.title: Smart paste converts rich clipboard content into markdown while preserving plain-text paste
steps: Copy rich content from a browser or rich-text editor that includes headings, lists, links, and inline emphasis.
Paste into MarkFlow in WYSIWYG mode and confirm markdown source is synthesized instead of raw HTML or flattened plain text.
Use the plain-text paste path and confirm literal text or markdown source is inserted without rich-format conversion.

automated: Add clipboard transformation tests covering HTML-to-markdown conversion and plain-text paste fallback.
manual: Paste from a browser page and a rich editor, then inspect both the rendered result and the underlying markdown.

features.notes: Typora pastes HTML by converting it to Markdown by default and supports an explicit plain-text or Markdown-source paste path, while MarkFlow currently relies on default browser/CodeMirror paste behavior with no clipboard normalization layer. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Copy-and-Paste/ and https://support.typora.io/Quick-Start/.

features.title: Word count surfaces live document and selection stats without treating markdown syntax as words
steps: Open a markdown document with prose, lists, and code, then observe a live word-count surface in the editor chrome.
Edit the document and confirm word, line, character, and reading-time stats update continuously.
Select a range and confirm the selection count is shown separately while markdown formatting syntax is excluded from word totals.

automated: Add derived-metric tests for document and selection counts, excluding formatting-only markdown syntax from word totals.
manual: Verify counts on mixed prose and markdown syntax match the detailed count surface for both whole-document and selected text.

features.notes: Typora exposes live word count plus lines, characters, reading time, and selection counts, and explicitly excludes formatting-only markdown syntax from word totals. MarkFlow currently has no count surface or metric derivation in the editor UI. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Word-Count/ and https://support.typora.io/Quick-Start/.

features.title: Find and replace can search the active document without leaving the editing flow
steps: Open a markdown document with repeated terms across headings, prose, and list items.
Trigger find and replace from the editor and navigate between matches in both source and WYSIWYG mode.
Replace the current match and all matches, then confirm the underlying markdown updates without losing selection or corrupting surrounding syntax.

automated: Add focused editor tests for search panel open state, match navigation, and replace/replace-all behavior against the live CodeMirror document.
manual: Verify case-sensitive, whole-word, and regex search options behave as expected on a mixed markdown document.

features.notes: Typora documents in-document search and replace, including case-sensitive, whole-word, and regex options. MarkFlow currently imports `searchKeymap` and `highlightSelectionMatches()` in `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx`, but it does not expose a verified search/replace workflow in the app or ledger. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Search/ and https://support.typora.io/What's-New-1.3/.

features.title: Dragging, pasting, or inserting local images creates markdown image references without breaking asset paths
steps: Open a saved markdown document and insert one or more local images via drag and drop, clipboard paste, or an explicit insert-image action.
Confirm MarkFlow inserts markdown image references at the caret and shows the existing inline preview when the caret leaves the image syntax.
If a destination rule for inserted images is configured, confirm the image file is copied and the `src` is rewritten predictably; otherwise confirm the original local path is referenced consistently.

automated: Add focused editor and desktop tests for drag/drop or clipboard image insertion, markdown `![alt](src)` generation, and destination-path rewriting for inserted local assets.
manual: Drag image files from the OS into the desktop app and paste an image from the clipboard, then verify the written markdown path and rendered preview both stay valid after save and reopen.

features.notes: MarkFlow already covers the core local-image insertion slice in `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx` and `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartPaste.ts`: dragging or pasting an image inserts markdown image syntax at the caret and emits desktop-bridge events for downstream file handling, while `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/linkDecoration.ts` renders the existing inline preview. Typora's broader image workflow still goes further with documented image-file management and upload actions such as Delete Image, Move/Copy Image To, and Upload Image / Upload All Local Images, which remain outside this verified slice. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Images/ and https://support.typora.io/Upload-Image/.

features.title: Fenced code blocks display language-aware syntax highlighting in both WYSIWYG and source modes
steps: Open a document with fenced code blocks annotated with common language identifiers such as `js`, `ts`, `python`, `bash`, and `json`.
Move the caret outside each block in WYSIWYG mode and confirm token-level syntax colors appear inside the rendered block.
Enter the block and confirm the source text remains fully editable with the same syntax-highlight colors applied in-place.

automated: Add `codeBlockDecoration.test.ts` cases verifying that language-tagged blocks receive a highlight decoration class and that no-language blocks fall back to plain styling.
manual: Visually confirm at least three language modes (JavaScript, Python, shell) render distinct token colors without corrupting the markdown fence delimiters.

features.notes: CodeMirror 6 ships `@codemirror/language` and a language-description registry; `StreamLanguage` adapters and `@codemirror/lang-*` packages can be lazy-loaded per fence language identifier. This makes code blocks meaningfully readable for technical writers and engineers, one of MarkFlow's primary target audiences.

features.title: Smart typography auto-converts ASCII punctuation to typographic equivalents as the user types
steps: Type straight double and single quotes in prose context and confirm they become curly/smart quotes.
Type `--` and `---` in prose and confirm they become en-dash and em-dash respectively.
Verify that straight quotes and hyphens inside code spans and fenced blocks are never transformed.

automated: Add `smartInput.test.ts` cases covering quote-pairing substitution, dash ligatures, and code-range exclusion.
manual: Write a paragraph of prose with dialogue and dashes; confirm the exported markdown contains proper Unicode punctuation while code samples are untouched.

features.notes: Typora enables smart quotes and smart dashes under Preferences > Editor. Implementing this as an additional CodeMirror transaction filter in `smartInput.ts` alongside the existing auto-pair logic keeps the behavior opt-in per settings. Critical for academics and technical writers aiming for publication-quality output.

features.title: Auto-save periodically writes the live buffer to disk so no work is lost on unexpected quit
steps: Open and edit a named file and let the auto-save interval elapse without explicitly saving.
Force-quit the app and reopen it; confirm the last auto-saved content is present.
Confirm the interval and enable/disable toggle are configurable in preferences and that untitled buffers are written to a recovery location.

automated: Add desktop integration tests that spy on the file-write path and assert it fires within the configured interval after document mutations.
manual: Edit a file for 30 seconds without saving, kill the process, reopen, and verify content recovery.

features.notes: Typora auto-saves on configurable intervals and offers crash recovery. The desktop `fileManager.ts` already has async write logic; adding a debounced auto-save loop driven by CodeMirror `docChanged` events is a natural extension. Reduces the most common user data-loss scenario.

features.title: Open Quickly fuzzy-searches files in the current folder and recent locations
steps: Open a markdown file from a folder containing multiple documents, then press `Cmd`+`Shift`+`O` on macOS or `Ctrl`+`P` on Windows/Linux.
Type part of a filename and confirm the quick-open overlay fuzzy-filters files from the current folder plus recent files.
Select a result with the keyboard and confirm the target file opens without disrupting the current editor session.

automated: Add renderer and desktop integration tests for quick-open panel state, keyboard navigation, fuzzy filtering against current-folder and recent-file entries, and file-open dispatch.
manual: Open a folder with at least 20 markdown files, reopen a few files, and confirm Quick Open can find both current-folder files and recent entries.

features.notes: Implemented with `packages/editor/src/components/QuickOpen.tsx`, `packages/editor/src/App.tsx`, and `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.ts`, which build a Quick Open overlay from the current document folder plus recent files tracked by the desktop shell. This verified slice matches Typora's core quick-open workflow for current-folder and recent-file lookup; pinned folders/locations remain outside the shipped scope. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Search/ and https://support.typora.io/File-Management/.

features.title: Admonition and callout blocks render with distinct icons and colors while preserving blockquote markdown
steps: Write blockquotes using GitHub-style callout syntax such as `> [!NOTE]`, `> [!WARNING]`, `> [!TIP]`, `> [!IMPORTANT]`, and `> [!CAUTION]`.
Move the caret out of each callout block in WYSIWYG mode and confirm a styled, icon-labelled callout widget replaces the raw blockquote.
Edit the callout text and confirm the type label, the `> ` prefix markers, and the body content all remain intact in the underlying markdown.

automated: Add `blockquoteDecoration.test.ts` cases for each callout type, verifying icon class, color class, and source round-trip fidelity.
manual: Visually confirm each callout type renders a distinct icon and accent color, and that switching between types updates the rendered widget without breaking the markdown.

features.notes: Typora added GitHub-style alert/callout rendering in Typora 1.8 and its Markdown Reference now lists the same five blockquote forms (`NOTE`, `TIP`, `IMPORTANT`, `WARNING`, `CAUTION`). MarkFlow currently styles generic blockquotes and horizontal rules only, so this remains a real parity gap that can be implemented as a specialization of the existing `blockquoteDecoration.ts` path. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.8/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Split view shows live source and rendered preview side-by-side and keeps them synchronized
steps: Toggle split view from the toolbar or command palette and confirm the window divides into a source pane on the left and a rendered preview pane on the right.
Edit markdown in the source pane and confirm the preview updates in near-real-time.
Scroll in either pane and confirm the other pane synchronizes scroll position proportionally.

automated: Add component tests verifying the split layout renders, that source mutations trigger preview updates within one render cycle, and that pane-resize handles adjust layout correctly.
manual: Open a long document in split mode and confirm scroll sync, inline math, and mermaid diagrams render correctly in the preview pane.

features.notes: Split view is a power-user mode between pure WYSIWYG and pure source editing. The preview pane can reuse the existing markdown rendering pipeline (decorations applied to a read-only CodeMirror view, or a simple `marked`/`remark` HTML output into an iframe) without duplicating the core editor. Particularly useful when writing technical docs that rely heavily on math, diagrams, or tables.

features.title: Heading sections can be folded and unfolded to hide document subtrees while editing
steps: Open a long document with multiple heading levels and observe fold indicators in the left gutter.
Click a fold indicator next to an H2 and confirm all content up to the next same-level heading collapses to a single summary line.
Unfold the section and confirm the full content is restored without losing any characters.

automated: Add CodeMirror fold-range tests verifying that heading fold ranges cover the correct line spans and that fold/unfold state-cycles produce identical document content.
manual: Fold and unfold nested headings on a real document; confirm fold state persists across WYSIWYG↔source mode toggles.

features.notes: CodeMirror 6 ships `@codemirror/language` fold support via `foldEffect`, `unfoldEffect`, and `foldGutter`. Defining heading-aware fold ranges (fold from heading line to the line before the next equal-or-higher heading) and wiring them to the gutter gives long-document writers a powerful navigation shortcut with minimal implementation risk.

features.title: Superscript and subscript syntax renders inline without mutating source
steps: Write inline superscript with `^text^` and subscript with `~text~` syntax in a WYSIWYG document.
Move the caret away from the marked span and confirm the rendered `<sup>` or `<sub>` widget appears with delimiters hidden.
Re-enter the span and confirm the raw delimiters reappear and are editable without corruption.

automated: Add `inlineDecorations.test.tsx` cases for superscript and subscript spanning, delimiter-hide behavior, and cursor-reveal round trips.
manual: Write a chemistry formula and a footnote reference using both syntaxes and verify rendered output is legible in WYSIWYG mode.

features.notes: Superscript (`^`) and subscript (`~`) are standard Pandoc and Typora Markdown extensions heavily used by academics writing chemistry, physics, and mathematics prose. Implementing them in `inlineDecorations.ts` using the same cursor-aware replace-decoration pattern as bold/italic requires minimal net-new code.

features.title: Inline HTML blocks pass through the WYSIWYG renderer without being escaped or stripped
steps: Insert raw HTML blocks such as `<details>`, `<summary>`, `<div>`, and plain `<span>` into a markdown document.
Render in WYSIWYG mode and confirm the HTML is parsed and rendered rather than escaped as literal text.
Edit the raw HTML source and confirm the rendered output updates and the surrounding markdown is unaffected.

automated: Add decoration tests for the currently supported safe-tag subset, including block containers and plain inline spans with attributes stripped.
manual: Write a `<details>/<summary>` collapsible block plus plain `<div>`/`<span>` HTML and verify they render correctly in WYSIWYG mode without relying on attributes.

features.notes: Implemented in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/inlineHtmlDecoration.ts`, but the current renderer is intentionally limited to a sanitized safe-tag subset (`details`, `summary`, `div`, `span`, `p`, `br`, `strong`, `em`, `b`, `i`, `ul`, `ol`, `li`) with all attributes stripped. That covers the verified collapsible/details-style HTML slice, but it does not provide Typora's broader media/embed support for tags such as `<video>`, `<audio>`, or `<iframe>`. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Media/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Spell checking underlines misspelled words in prose and offers inline correction suggestions
steps: Type a deliberate misspelling in a prose paragraph and confirm a red squiggly underline appears beneath the word.
Right-click or invoke the correction shortcut and confirm a list of candidate corrections is offered.
Verify that words inside fenced code blocks, inline code, URLs, and YAML front matter are excluded from spell checking.

automated: Add unit tests for spell-check range exclusion (code, URL, front matter) to prevent false positives in non-prose regions.
manual: Write a document mixing prose, code, and a URL, confirm only prose words receive squiggle decorations, and accept one correction suggestion.

features.notes: Spell checking is expected by writers and academics. In Electron the browser's native `spellcheck` attribute can be used on the contenteditable layer, or a CodeMirror linter powered by `nspell` or a native OS API can provide range-aware suggestions while excluding code and metadata regions.

features.title: Wikilinks (`[[page]]`) resolve to internal document links and navigate within the active vault
steps: Type `[[filename]]` or `[[filename|display text]]` in a document inside an open vault folder.
Move the caret out of the wikilink and confirm a rendered link widget appears.
Cmd/Ctrl+click the widget and confirm the referenced document opens (or a new-file prompt appears if it doesn't exist).

automated: Add decoration tests for wikilink parsing, alias rendering, broken-link visual state, and click-dispatch to the file-open IPC path.
manual: Create a two-file vault, write wikilinks between them, and verify bidirectional navigation works without corrupting either file's source.

features.notes: Wikilink syntax is the cornerstone of Obsidian and Roam-style networked note-taking, making MarkFlow competitive in the knowledge-management space. Implementing wikilink decoration in `linkDecoration.ts` and resolving targets via the desktop file tree covers the Typora/Obsidian overlap audience and sets the foundation for backlinks (a Phase 4 feature).

features.title: Vault sidebar shows a file tree for the active folder and supports open, rename, and delete
steps: Open a folder as a vault from File > Open Folder and confirm a sidebar tree panel appears listing all markdown and asset files.
Click a file in the sidebar and confirm the editor opens it, replacing or adding a tab for the new document.
Rename and delete files from the sidebar context menu and confirm the filesystem reflects the changes.

automated: Add desktop integration tests for vault open IPC, directory scan, file-open from tree, and rename/delete operations.
manual: Open a folder with at least 20 markdown files, verify the tree is navigable, and confirm sidebar file operations match the OS filesystem.

features.notes: Verified minimum file-sidebar parity: MarkFlow can open a folder, show a file tree, and perform open/rename/delete actions from the sidebar. Typora's current file-management docs still describe broader folder-side capabilities that remain outside this verified slice, including File List view, auto/manual refresh, sorting/grouping controls, drag-move, recent-location pinning, and open-in-new-window behavior. Sources: https://support.typora.io/File-Management/ and https://support.typora.io/Quick-Start/.

features.title: Vim keybinding mode provides modal editing with normal, insert, and visual commands
steps: Enable vim mode from preferences or the command palette and confirm the editor enters Normal mode with a block cursor.
Navigate with `hjkl`, enter Insert mode with `i`, and return to Normal with `Escape`.
Perform a `dd` delete-line, `yy` yank-line, and `p` paste and confirm the document updates correctly without corrupting markdown syntax.

automated: Add integration tests for mode transitions (Normal→Insert→Normal), common motion/operator combinations, and `Escape` behavior that does not conflict with MarkFlow WYSIWYG toggle.
manual: Write and edit a full markdown document using only vim keybindings and confirm no loss of MarkFlow-specific shortcuts (mode toggle, find/replace).

features.notes: CodeMirror 6 ships a battle-tested `@codemirror/vim` extension that provides modal vim editing. Enabling it as a preferences toggle (following VS Code and Obsidian's pattern) costs negligible bundle size via dynamic import and is highly requested by engineers who are a primary MarkFlow target audience.

features.title: Reading mode renders the document as fully static HTML without an editable code surface
steps: Toggle reading mode from the toolbar or shortcut and confirm the editor surface becomes non-editable and all markdown syntax is replaced by styled HTML.
Confirm all WYSIWYG decorations (headings, math, diagrams, callouts, images) render in reading mode without the cursor-reveal behavior.
Return to editing mode and confirm the document state, selection, and undo history are preserved exactly.

automated: Add tests that verify the reading-mode extension disables all input handling, applies all decoration sets unconditionally, and that toggling back to edit mode restores a live CodeMirror state.
manual: Switch to reading mode on a document containing math, a mermaid diagram, a table, and an admonition block; confirm each renders without any syntax marker visible.

features.notes: MarkFlow's current WYSIWYG mode still reveals raw syntax when the caret is inside a decorated range. Reading mode removes the caret entirely and applies all decorations unconditionally, giving users a clean finished-document view for proofreading and sharing. This is the third point in the source ↔ WYSIWYG ↔ reading triad that Typora expresses as its signature UX.

features.title: Highlight spans (`==text==`) render inline without mutating source
steps: Write inline highlight markup such as `==important==` in a WYSIWYG document.
Move the caret away from the span and confirm the delimiter markers hide while the text renders with highlight styling.
Move the caret back into the span and confirm the raw `==` delimiters reappear for editing without corrupting the underlying markdown.

manual: Optional in-app spot-check: write several highlighted phrases adjacent to bold or italic spans and confirm neighboring inline syntax remains editable and visually distinct.

features.notes: Implemented in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/inlineDecorations.ts` as a document-preserving inline decoration pass that hides `==` delimiters outside the caret, restores raw source when the caret enters the span, and skips inline/fenced code ranges. Automated coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/inlineDecorations.test.tsx` verifies delimiter hiding, caret reveal, code-context exclusion, compatibility with neighboring bold/italic spans, and markdown round trips. Source: https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Global search queries content across files in the opened folder and jumps to exact matches
steps: Open a folder containing multiple markdown files and invoke global search from the sidebar or with `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`Shift`+`F`.
Search for a phrase, regex, or `#tag`-style token and confirm matching files plus snippet locations appear across the folder.
Select a result and confirm MarkFlow opens the correct file, scrolls to the match, and highlights the selected occurrence.

automated: Add desktop and renderer integration tests for folder indexing, cross-file search filters (case, whole-word, regex), and result navigation into the correct file and text range.
manual: Open a folder with matches in several files, run global search from the sidebar, and confirm selecting results jumps to the right file and text range.

features.notes: Typora's Search and File Management docs describe sidebar-backed global search across files, including case-sensitive, whole-word, and regex toggles, plus literal `#tag` discovery across the opened folder. MarkFlow currently only ships current-document find/replace under MF-028; repo search shows no folder index, sidebar search UI, or cross-file result navigation, so this is a genuinely missing Typora capability. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Search/ and https://support.typora.io/File-Management/.

features.title: Export pipeline integrates with Pandoc to generate DOCX, EPUB, and LaTeX artifacts from the active document
steps: Ensure Pandoc is installed on the host system or configured in MarkFlow.
Open a markdown document with headings, lists, code, and links.
Export the document to DOCX, EPUB, and LaTeX formats.
Confirm the exported artifacts preserve document structure and styling.

features.notes: Typora relies on Pandoc as a bridge for exporting to advanced formats like Word (.docx), EPUB, LaTeX, and OpenOffice. Implementation complete: `FileManager.exportPandoc()` in packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.ts handles CLI invocation with correct args (--standalone for LaTeX), IPC handlers wired in registerIpcHandlers(), preload exposes exportDocx/exportEpub/exportLatex, menu items trigger export-docx/epub/latex actions, and App.tsx handlePandocExport() routes these to the API. Fixed in this run: electron mock missing `app` in fileManager.test.ts (blocking smoke), and missing handlePandocExport function + payload.action reference error in App.tsx. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Export/

features.title: Paragraph shortcuts convert the current line between heading levels 1-6 and plain paragraph text
steps: Place the caret on a plain paragraph line and press `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`1` through `6` to apply the matching heading level.
Place the caret on an existing heading line and press `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`0` to convert it back to a plain paragraph.
Confirm the current line changes in-place, other lines remain untouched, and the underlying markdown source stays editable.

features.notes: Typora documents paragraph shortcuts for Heading 1-6 (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`1..6`) and Paragraph (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`0`), and its Markdown Reference calls out typing heading markers or using the shortcut family as first-class authoring behavior. MarkFlow now handles these shortcuts through `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts`, which rewrites only the active line's markdown source while preserving the rest of the document unchanged; regression coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` verifies paragraph-to-heading conversion, heading-to-paragraph reset, and active-line-only edits. Verified on 2026-04-11 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` plus `node scripts/harness/verify.mjs`. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Virtual rendering limits DOM nodes to a viewport window for files over 5 000 lines

Automated verification passed on 2026-04-13 (`pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- --grep incremental`, targeted editor tests, `./harness/init.sh --smoke`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`). Not tested: the manual 180k-line parse-scope console check and sub-16 ms latency validation on real M-series hardware.

features.title: Background indexer builds a symbol table for headings and anchors without blocking the UI thread
steps: Open a 180k-line document.

Automated coverage in `src/editor/__tests__/indexer.test.ts`, `src/editor/__tests__/outline.test.ts`, `src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`, and `src/__tests__/App.test.tsx` passed on 2026-04-15 with the listed editor test command, plus `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build`, and `pnpm harness:verify`. `passes` remains false because the manual 180k-line typing/no-lag verification is still pending.

features.title: Outline panel lists all headings with live scroll-sync and click-to-jump navigation
steps: Open any document with multiple heading levels.
Confirm the outline panel updates as headings are added or removed.

Scroll the editor and confirm the active heading in the panel tracks the viewport.

manual: Manual scroll-sync check with a multi-section document.

Automated coverage in `src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`, `src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`, and `src/editor/__tests__/outline.test.ts` passed on 2026-04-15 with the listed editor test command. `passes` remains false because the manual desktop scroll-sync check is still pending.

features.title: Chunk-based file loader streams large files in 64 KB chunks to avoid blocking the main thread on open
steps: Open a 200 MB markdown file via File > Open.
Confirm the first screen-full of content is visible within 1 second.

Not-tested: manual open-time/OOM check on 200 MB file.

features.title: Fuzzy document-wide search highlights all matches and jumps between them with keyboard shortcuts
steps: Press Cmd+F to open the search bar.
Type a query and confirm all matches are highlighted within 200 ms on a 180k-line file.
Press Enter / Shift+Enter to cycle through matches.
Confirm match count badge is accurate.

manual: Search for a common word in the large-file fixture; count highlighted matches.

features.notes: Run search in a Web Worker using a regex; stream decoration ranges back to viewport-visible lines first.

features.title: Find-and-replace supports regex mode, case-sensitivity toggle, and whole-word matching
steps: Press Cmd/Ctrl+H to open the find-and-replace panel from the editor.
Enable regex mode, enter a pattern, and confirm only regex-matching spans are highlighted.
Toggle case-sensitivity and confirm the match set changes accordingly.
Toggle whole-word matching, then use Replace All and confirm all intended occurrences are substituted without corrupting surrounding text.

manual: Regex replace on a known fixture; diff the result against expected output.

features.notes: Typora's Search doc says Cmd/Ctrl+H toggles Find and Replace in the current file and that regex replacement supports `$0`, `$1`, ... capture references, alongside case-sensitive, whole-word, and regexp toggles. MarkFlow now exposes the existing CodeMirror search panel through an explicit `Mod-h` binding in `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx`, focuses the replace field when opened from the editor, and relies on the built-in panel controls for regex, match-case, whole-word, and replace/replace-all behavior. Focused regression coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/findReplace.test.ts` and `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` now exercises live panel creation, query toggle commits, match navigation, regex capture-group replacement, exact-case replacement, whole-word replacement, and the `Cmd/Ctrl+H` entry point. Automated verification passed on 2026-04-16 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor exec vitest run src/editor/__tests__/findReplace.test.ts src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` and `pnpm harness:verify`. `passes` remains false because the manual regex-replace fixture check is still pending. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Search/ and https://support.typora.io/What's-New-1.3/.

features.title: Memory-mapped read path allows random-access navigation in files larger than available RAM
steps: Open a synthetic 2 GB markdown file.
Jump to line 1 000 000 via the Go-to-Line dialog.
Confirm the target line is displayed within 2 seconds.
Confirm resident memory usage stays below 512 MB.

manual: Monitor Activity Monitor RSS while jumping to arbitrary positions.

features.notes: Electron main-process reads a sliding window; renderer requests line ranges via IPC.

features.title: Go-to-line dialog jumps the caret and viewport to an arbitrary line number instantly
steps: Press Cmd/Ctrl+L, enter a line number, and confirm the caret moves to that line.
Confirm the viewport scrolls so the target line is visible near the top of the editor.

The app computes the target document offset from the requested line number, clamps out-of-range input to the document bounds, and reuses the existing `MarkFlowEditor` navigation request path so `EditorView.dispatch` sets the caret and runs `scrollIntoView` without recreating the editor. Automated coverage lives in `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx` and passed on 2026-04-12 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- --grep go-to-line`, plus `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build` and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`. `passes` remains false only because the required manual large-document shortcut smoke test is still pending.

features.title: Multi-cursor editing allows placing cursors on non-contiguous lines and editing them simultaneously
steps: Alt-click to place additional cursors on three separate lines.
Type text and confirm it is inserted at every cursor simultaneously.
Press Escape and confirm cursors collapse to a single primary caret.

Automated coverage lives in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` and passed on 2026-04-12 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- --grep multi-cursor`, plus `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build` and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`. `passes` remains false only because the manual Alt-click interaction check is still pending.

features.title: Column (block) selection mode lets users select a rectangular region across multiple lines
steps: Alt+Shift+drag to create a rectangular selection across 5 lines.
Type a replacement string and confirm it replaces the selected column on every line.
Confirm undo restores all lines atomically.

features.title: Undo history is capped at 500 steps and compressed for large-file sessions to stay under 256 MB
steps: Make 600 distinct edits in a large file.
Confirm undo walks back 500 steps and stops cleanly.
Confirm heap size does not exceed 256 MB after 600 edits.

manual: Monitor heap in DevTools Memory snapshot after 600 edits.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-12 in `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx` and `packages/editor/src/editor/historyLimit.ts`. The editor now configures CodeMirror history with a 500-event target and prunes serialized `historyField` state back to the newest 500 change events whenever the native branch grows past the cap, which keeps undo depth exact even though CodeMirror's built-in history only exposes a retention floor (`minDepth`) instead of a true max depth. Automated coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` proves that 600 isolated edits settle at exactly 500 undoable events and that the 501st undo stops cleanly; it passed on 2026-04-12 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- --grep "caps undo history"`, plus `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build` and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`. `passes` remains false because the manual heap-size verification for large-file sessions is still pending.

features.title: Auto-save writes a recovery checkpoint every 30 seconds without blocking the editor
steps: Edit a document without manually saving.
Wait 30 seconds and confirm a .markflow-recovery file is written to the system temp directory.
Simulate a crash; reopen MarkFlow and confirm the recovery prompt appears.
Accept recovery and confirm the checkpoint content is restored.

manual: Kill the process after 35 seconds; reopen and accept recovery.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-15 in `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.ts`, `packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts`, `packages/editor/src/App.tsx`, `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.test.ts`, and `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx`. The desktop main process now keeps the latest dirty-document draft in memory, debounces `.markflow-recovery` writes into the system temp directory after 30 seconds of idle time, records clean versus unclean shutdown state, and only surfaces the recovery prompt after an unclean exit. Automated verification passed on 2026-04-15 and again on 2026-04-16 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/desktop test:run -- --grep auto-save` and `pnpm harness:verify`. Manual crash/relaunch verification completed on 2026-04-16 in a live Electron session against `/tmp/markflow-mf060.vh4jMh/recovery.md`: Chromium CDP edited the open file without saving, waited 35 seconds, observed `/var/folders/dl/qdq_vh116gl1yjbd8pxk_bd00000gn/T/.markflow-recovery` containing `checkpoint-from-cdp`, killed the Electron process to preserve `{"cleanExit":false}` in `/Users/pprp/Library/Application Support/@markflow/desktop/.markflow-recovery-session.json`, relaunched MarkFlow, captured the recovery prompt text `Recover the auto-saved changes for recovery.md from 4/16/2026, 7:44:39 AM?` via `Page.javascriptDialogOpening`, accepted it with `Page.handleJavaScriptDialog`, and confirmed the restored checkpoint content plus dirty-state indicator in the renderer.

features.title: Lazy image loading defers off-screen image decoding until the image enters the viewport
steps: Open a document with 500 embedded images.

Markdown image widgets now create their `` without a `src`, attach an `IntersectionObserver` rooted to the editor scroller, and only assign the resolved source once the widget nears the viewport; if the widget is removed before loading, the observer disconnects so stale off-screen work is not retained. Focused lazy-image coverage passed in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/lazyImage.test.tsx`, and the required `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- --grep lazy-image`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint`, and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build` commands also passed on 2026-04-16. `passes` remains false because the required DevTools/CPU-throttled scroll verification could not be truthfully completed from this terminal session.

features.title: Increase/Decrease Heading Level shortcuts promote or demote the current heading by one level
steps: Place the caret on a heading line such as `## Heading` and press `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`=`.
