features.title: Paragraph shortcuts convert the current line into quote, ordered-list, and unordered-list blocks
steps: Place the caret on a plain paragraph line and press Typora's Quote shortcut (`Ctrl`+`Shift`+`Q` on Windows/Linux, `Cmd`+`Option`+`Q` on macOS), then confirm only the active line rewrites to `> ` markdown.

Repeat with Typora's Ordered List shortcut (`Ctrl`+`Shift`+`[` / `Cmd`+`Option`+`O`) and Unordered List shortcut (`Ctrl`+`Shift`+`]` / `Cmd`+`Option`+`U`), confirming the active line rewrites in place to `1. ` and `- ` style list markup without mutating adjacent lines.
Press Typora's Paragraph shortcut (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`0`) on each converted line and confirm MarkFlow returns that line to plain paragraph text while preserving surrounding content and caret position.

manual: Open the desktop editor and confirm the Quote, Ordered List, Unordered List, and Paragraph shortcuts rewrite only the active line in both source and WYSIWYG modes, while existing Enter-driven list continuation from MF-006 still behaves normally after conversion.

features.notes: Verified on 2026-04-12. Typora's Quote (`Ctrl+Shift+Q` / `Cmd+Option+Q`), Ordered List (`Ctrl+Shift+[` / `Cmd+Option+O`), Unordered List (`Ctrl+Shift+]` / `Cmd+Option+U`), and Paragraph (`Mod-0`) shortcuts all work correctly in `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts`. Quote/list toggles on active line, Mod-0 strips prefixes back to plain text, task-list lines preserved. Coverage in `MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` and `smartInput.test.ts`; 231 tests pass. Not-tested: manual desktop shortcut check in Electron shell.

features.title: Style shortcuts wrap the current selection for bold, italic, underline, and links without leaving the editor flow
steps: Select inline prose in the editor, press Typora's Bold and Italic shortcuts (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`B` and `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`I`), and confirm only the selected range is wrapped in `**...**` or `*...*` source while the rendered styling appears again after the caret leaves the span.
Select text, press Typora's Underline shortcut (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`U`), and confirm MarkFlow wraps just that range in `<u>...</u>` without corrupting adjacent markdown or leaving the editor.

Select text, press Typora's Link shortcut (`Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`K`), and confirm MarkFlow scaffolds `[selected text](url)` around the selection so the existing link rendering/navigation path can take over once the destination is edited.

automated: Add focused editor shortcut tests covering `Mod-b`, `Mod-i`, `Mod-u`, and `Mod-k`, including collapsed vs selected ranges and markdown/HTML round trips.
manual: Open the desktop editor in both source and WYSIWYG modes, use the four shortcuts on real selections, and confirm the inserted wrappers stay minimal while the existing floating toolbar behavior remains unchanged.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-12. Added `Mod-b` (bold), `Mod-i` (italic), `Mod-u` (underline), and `Mod-k` (link) keyboard shortcuts to `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts`. Each shortcut wraps the selected text with the appropriate markdown wrapper, or inserts a wrapper with placeholder text and selects the content when the cursor has no selection. Link shortcuts scaffold `[text](url)` with the cursor positioned on the URL. Automated coverage lives in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts` (direct handler tests) and `MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` (component-level bold and link shortcut tests). All 217 tests pass.

features.title: Alternate LaTeX delimiters `\(...\)` and `\[...\]` render as math in WYSIWYG mode
steps: Open markdown containing inline `\(a^2+b^2=c^2\)` and block `\[\int_0^1 x^2 dx\]` math delimiters.
Move the caret out of each expression and confirm it renders through the existing KaTeX path.
Re-enter the expression and confirm raw source delimiters stay editable without corruption.

manual: In desktop editor, verify mixed `$...$`, `$$...$$`, `\(...\)`, and `\[...\]` expressions in one document render consistently.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-15 in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/mathDecoration.ts` by extending the existing KaTeX decoration path to recognize inline `\(...\)`, single-line `\[...\]`, and multi-line `\[` / `\]` display blocks while preserving caret reveal and code-range exclusion. `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/mathDecoration.test.ts` now covers alternate-delimiter rendering, caret-inside source reveal, multi-line block handling, and fenced/inline-code exclusion. Automated verification passed on 2026-04-15 with the listed math test command plus `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint` and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build`. `passes` remains false because the manual desktop mixed-delimiter verification is still pending. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.11/ and https://support.typora.io/Math/.

features.title: Table editing hotkeys support row/column insertion and row movement without raw markdown surgery
steps: Place the caret in a rendered markdown table and trigger table row/column insert actions from shortcuts or command palette.
Use Typora-style move-row commands to move the current row up/down and confirm table structure remains valid.
Confirm undo/redo restores table structure atomically after each table edit command.

automated: Add command-level tests in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/tableDecoration.test.ts` (or dedicated table command tests) for insert/delete/move row operations preserving pipe-table syntax.
manual: Use table editing commands repeatedly in a 20-row table and confirm no malformed rows or delimiter drift.

features.notes: Typora documents richer table editing commands (insert/delete row/column) and release notes mention move-table-row shortcuts, while MarkFlow currently provides table rendering/styling only. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Table-Editing/ and https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.11/.

features.title: Rendered markdown tables support interactive column resizing with persisted alignment
steps: Hover table header boundaries and confirm a resize affordance appears in WYSIWYG mode.
Drag to resize a column and confirm visible widths update immediately without corrupting markdown content.
Save and reopen the document, then confirm alignment/width intent survives via markdown-compatible serialization strategy.

automated: Add integration tests for resize-handle interactions and resulting markdown persistence behavior in editor table tests.
manual: Resize multiple columns in a wide table and verify keyboard navigation plus selection behavior still works.

features.notes: Typora documents draggable table column resizing; MarkFlow `tableDecoration.ts` currently decorates rows/cells but has no resize handles or width persistence path. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Table-Editing/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Image insertion honors Typora-style destination/root preferences for dropped and pasted assets
steps: Set `typora-copy-images-to` and `typora-root-url` in YAML front matter, then paste/drag local images into the editor.
Confirm inserted markdown paths are rewritten according to destination/root rules and previews still resolve.
Repeat with no YAML preferences and confirm fallback insertion behavior matches current MF-029 flow.

automated: Extend image insertion tests in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartPaste.test.ts` and `linkDecoration.test.tsx` for YAML-driven destination rewriting and root URL resolution.
manual: Create two notes with different front-matter image preferences and verify each rewrites image paths independently.

features.notes: MarkFlow already stores YAML metadata and basic image insertion, but does not consume Typora image preference keys like `typora-copy-images-to` / `typora-root-url` during insert flow. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Images/ and https://support.typora.io/YAML/.

features.title: Spellcheck settings allow language selection and custom dictionary behavior in-editor
steps: Open spellcheck settings and choose a non-default language dictionary for the active document or app profile.
Add a domain-specific term to a custom dictionary and confirm it is no longer flagged as misspelled.
Switch back to default language and confirm language-specific misspelling detection changes accordingly.

automated: Add tests around spellcheck configuration plumbing (language selection + per-profile dictionary persistence) while preserving existing exclusion ranges.
manual: Toggle between two dictionaries (for example en-US and de-DE) and verify misspelling highlights differ on the same text.

features.notes: Typora release notes continue adding UI language support and mature spellcheck workflows typically include configurable dictionaries; MarkFlow currently enables browser spellcheck with exclusion ranges but no exposed language/dictionary controls in editor scope. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.13/ and https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.10/.

features.title: Image drag-to-resize handles update width/height in markdown source
steps: Render a markdown image in WYSIWYG mode and hover to reveal corner/edge resize handles.
Drag a handle and confirm the preview resizes live without corrupting markdown syntax.
Release the handle and confirm the underlying source gains a `{width=...}` / `=WIDTHxHEIGHT` attribute (Typora-compatible) that survives reopen.

automated: Add editor-level tests for resize-handle gesture emulation in `linkDecoration.test.tsx` that assert markdown is rewritten with image size attributes.
manual: Drag-resize three consecutive images of different aspect ratios and confirm both inline preview and saved source match.

features.notes: Typora exposes interactive image resize via corner handles and persists width through `{width=...}` attribute syntax. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Images/ and https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.1/.

features.title: Emoji shortcode autocomplete expands `:name:` into Unicode glyphs
steps: Type `:smi` and confirm a completion popup lists matching emoji shortcodes with glyph previews.
Accept a suggestion and confirm the inserted text is the Unicode glyph (not the shortcode) while source mode still preserves user edits.
Type an unknown shortcode and confirm the completion dismisses gracefully without mutating the document.

manual: Insert several emoji in the middle of prose and confirm caret position remains correct after acceptance.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-16 with a small CodeMirror autocomplete source in `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/emojiAutocomplete.ts`, wired into `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx`. This run ships a curated starter set of common Typora-style emoji shortcodes: typing `:` plus a known prefix now offers matching completions with glyph previews via completion detail text, accepting a suggestion inserts the Unicode glyph itself, and unknown shortcodes return no completions so the popup dismisses without changing the document. Focused regression coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/emojiAutocomplete.test.ts` passed with the listed Vitest command, and targeted lint, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build`, plus `pnpm harness:verify` also passed. `passes` remains false because the required manual caret-position check in live prose is still pending. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Command palette (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P) invokes any editor or desktop action by name
steps: Press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P and confirm a searchable command overlay opens centered above the editor.
Filter by prefix and confirm matching commands (toggle WYSIWYG, toggle outline, export HTML, insert table, etc.) appear ranked by relevance.
Execute a selected command and confirm the editor performs it and the overlay closes without losing selection or scroll position.

automated: Add `commandPalette.test.tsx` asserting registration, fuzzy filtering, and execution dispatch against a representative action registry.
manual: Exercise five categories of commands (view, insert, export, navigation, edit) through the palette without touching menus.

features.notes: Typora introduced a command palette in later 1.x releases. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.7/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Copy writes rich clipboard formats, while Copy as Markdown and Copy as HTML Code expose source formats
steps: Select text containing bold, a link, and inline code.
Run the normal `Copy` action and paste into both a rich-text target and a plain-text target; confirm the default clipboard payload carries rendered rich content.
Run `Copy as Markdown` and confirm a plain-text paste contains raw markdown source, then run `Copy as HTML Code` and confirm a plain-text paste contains HTML source.

manual: Paste the same selection into a rich-text app and a plain-text/code editor to confirm all three copy paths behave like Typora.

features.notes: Implemented with explicit `copy`, `copy-as-markdown`, and `copy-as-html-code` menu actions, a bounded preload clipboard bridge, and a minimal renderer-side markdown selection serializer for bold, emphasis, links, inline code, and paragraph/line-break structure. Automated coverage now asserts default copy writes `text/html` plus rendered plaintext, while the source-oriented actions write markdown text or HTML fragment text through `packages/editor/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx` and `packages/desktop/src/main/menu.test.ts`. `passes` remains false until a manual cross-app clipboard check confirms real Electron clipboard behavior in both rich-text and plain-text targets. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Copy-and-Paste/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Paste as plain text shortcut strips rich formatting before insertion
steps: Copy rich-text content from an external source.
Press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+V in the editor and confirm the inserted text contains no markdown conversion and no HTML residue.
Repeat with Cmd/Ctrl+V and confirm smart-paste conversion (MF-026) still applies on the default paste path.

manual: Paste from three rich sources (Word, webpage, VS Code) with and without the shortcut and compare results.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-15 in `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartPaste.ts` by tracking a short-lived Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+V intent window so the next paste uses `text/plain` even when the clipboard also carries HTML, while preserving the default HTML→Markdown smart-paste path and existing image-paste priority. Focused coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartPaste.test.ts` now verifies default rich-text conversion, plain-text shortcut bypass, one-shot shortcut reset, and image-paste precedence. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Copy-Paste/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Clear formatting command removes inline markup from the current selection
steps: Select a span containing bold, italic, inline code, and a link.
Run Clear Formatting (menu, palette, or Cmd/Ctrl+\).
Confirm the selection becomes plain text while surrounding formatting is untouched.

automated: Add a `clearFormatting.test.ts` that covers emphasis, code, strikethrough, highlight, and link unwrapping across ranges.
manual: Run the action on nested formatting (e.g. bold+italic+link) and confirm every layer strips cleanly.

features.notes: Typora's Format menu exposes Clear Formatting. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Move line and selection up/down shortcut reorders blocks without clipboard usage
steps: Place the caret on a line and press Alt+Up or Alt+Down — confirm the line swaps with the adjacent line while the caret stays on the moved line.
Select three lines and press Alt+Up or Alt+Down — confirm the selection moves as a contiguous block while preserving the moved selection state.
Verify undo/redo returns the document to the prior state atomically.

manual: Reorder a numbered markdown list with Alt+Up/Down in the desktop editor and confirm the list structure/numbering still matches the intended order.

features.notes: Implemented and tightened on 2026-04-15. Typora 1.11 documents Alt+Up/Down for moving paragraphs or rows. MarkFlow inherits CodeMirror's default `Alt-ArrowUp` / `Alt-ArrowDown` `moveLineUp` and `moveLineDown` bindings through `packages/editor/src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx`, and `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` now directly covers single-line moves, contiguous block moves in both directions, and undo/redo atomicity. Automated verification passed with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor exec vitest run src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor lint -- src/editor/MarkFlowEditor.tsx src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`, and `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor build`. `passes` remains false because desktop validation for real numbered-list reordering is still pending. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/ and https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.11/.

features.title: Duplicate line or selection shortcut inserts a copy below without touching the clipboard
steps: Place caret on a line and press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+D — confirm the line is duplicated directly beneath and the caret lands on the copy.
Select multiple lines and repeat — confirm the block duplicates as a contiguous copy.
Confirm undo collapses the duplication into a single atomic step.

automated: Add keymap tests covering line and block duplication in `MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`.
manual: Duplicate a code fence and confirm both copies remain syntactically valid.

features.notes: Duplicate line is a common writing-tool shortcut paralleling Typora's editing ergonomics. Source: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Auto-pair brackets, quotes, backticks, and markdown delimiters on insertion
steps: Type `(`, `[`, `{`, `"`, `'`, and `` ` `` and confirm each emits a matching closing character with the caret placed between the pair.

Type `*` or `_` in an emphasis-friendly text position and confirm the editor inserts a matching closing delimiter, wraps selections with the typed delimiter, and Backspace removes an empty pair atomically.
Type `*` at the start of a line and `_` inside an identifier such as `snake_case`, and confirm those literal-entry cases are not intercepted by auto-pairing.

automated: Run `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts` to cover structural auto-pairs, context-sensitive `*`/`_` auto-pairs, selection wrapping, skip-over behavior, empty-pair backspace, start-of-line `*` list entry, and identifier underscore fallthrough.

features.notes: Completed on 2026-04-15. `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts` keeps unconditional pairing for structural delimiters and routes `*`/`_` through a markdown-specific key path: selections still wrap, paired delimiters still support skip-over and empty-pair backspace, but `*` falls through at line start so unordered-list entry still works and `_` falls through inside identifiers/plain text such as `snake_case`. `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts` exercises the real keymap path for both the paired and literal-entry cases. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Auto-Pair/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Image upload integration sends pasted/dropped images through a PicGo-compatible uploader
steps: Configure an image uploader endpoint (PicGo core / custom command) in preferences.
Paste or drop a local image and confirm it is uploaded, the returned URL is inserted as `![alt](https://...)`, and the local copy policy matches settings.
Simulate an upload failure and confirm the editor surfaces an error toast without losing the pasted asset.

automated: Add desktop-side tests for the uploader adapter (success, failure, timeout) and editor tests confirming the URL rewrite path.
manual: Upload three images through a real PicGo instance and verify each ends up in the remote store.

features.notes: Typora documents image upload via custom commands and PicGo. Source: https://support.typora.io/Upload-Image/.

features.title: Preferences dialog exposes editor, theme, export, and shortcut settings
steps: Open Preferences (Cmd/Ctrl+,) and confirm sections for Editor, Appearance, Export, and Shortcuts exist.
Change a representative setting from each section, close the dialog, and confirm the change persists across restart.
Reset to defaults and confirm every section returns to baseline without corrupting user content.

automated: Add desktop tests around preferences persistence (JSON read/write, migration, default reset).
manual: Walk through every preferences tab and verify each control is wired to real behavior.

features.notes: Typora ships a multi-tab Preferences window. Source: https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Reveal in Finder / Show in folder action opens the active file's location
steps: Open a saved markdown file.
Trigger the Reveal in Finder / Show in folder menu or palette action.
Confirm the OS file manager opens with the file highlighted; confirm the action is disabled for untitled/unsaved documents.

automated: pnpm --filter @markflow/desktop exec vitest run src/main/fileManager.test.ts src/main/menu.test.ts
manual: Exercise the action on macOS, Windows, and Linux builds.

features.notes: Implemented on 2026-04-15 in `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.ts`, `packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts`, `packages/desktop/src/main/menu.ts`, `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.test.ts`, and `packages/desktop/src/main/menu.test.ts`. The desktop File menu now exposes `Reveal in Finder` on macOS, `Show in File Explorer` on Windows, and `Show in Folder` elsewhere; it stays disabled until the current document has a saved path, rebuilds after open/new/Save As path transitions, and reveals the latest saved path through `shell.showItemInFolder`. Automated verification passed on 2026-04-15 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/desktop exec vitest run src/main/fileManager.test.ts src/main/menu.test.ts`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/desktop lint`, `pnpm --filter @markflow/desktop build`, and `pnpm harness:verify`. `passes` remains false because the required manual cross-platform OS file-manager verification is still pending. Sources: https://support.typora.io/File-Management/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Fullscreen and distraction-free modes hide window chrome and side panels
steps: Trigger fullscreen (F11 / Cmd+Ctrl+F) and confirm the OS window chrome is hidden.
Trigger distraction-free mode and confirm sidebars/outline/toolbars are hidden while the document stays centered.
Exit both modes and confirm prior panel visibility is restored exactly.

automated: Add tests asserting panel visibility state is saved before entering each mode and restored after exit.
manual: Alternate between fullscreen, distraction-free, and default view on a long document and confirm no flicker.

features.notes: Typora separates Fullscreen from a distraction-free Typewriter-style view. Source: https://support.typora.io/Menu/.

features.title: Heading auto-numbering toggle renders 1., 1.1, 1.2 prefixes via CSS counters
steps: Enable heading auto-numbering in preferences.
Open a document with nested H1–H4 headings and confirm numeric prefixes render in-editor, outline panel, and exports.
Disable the setting and confirm the markdown source never gains the numbers (only rendered output).

automated: Add style-layer tests asserting CSS counters produce the expected prefixes and that source text is untouched.
manual: Export the numbered document to PDF and HTML and confirm the numbering survives.

features.notes: Typora supports heading auto-numbering via `sidebar.heading` CSS counters and export settings. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Auto-Numbering/ and https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Math block equation auto-numbering labels display and resolve `\ref` references
steps: Enable math equation auto-numbering and write several display-math blocks with `\label{eq:foo}`.
Confirm each block renders with an incrementing `(n)` label on the right edge and that the label matches across WYSIWYG and source.
Reference the label via `\ref{eq:foo}` in prose and confirm the rendered number matches the originating equation.

automated: Add math tests asserting counter increments and reference resolution across multiple equations.
manual: Reorder two labeled equations and confirm references update consistently.

features.notes: Typora provides a math equation auto-numbering toggle that resolves `\label`/`\ref` pairs. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Math/ and https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Flowchart, sequence, and gantt fences render alongside Mermaid
steps: Author fenced blocks with languages `flow`, `sequence`, `mermaid` (sequence/gantt), and confirm each renders an inline diagram preview.
Introduce a syntax error in one block and confirm the editor surfaces a localized render error without breaking siblings.
Edit the source and confirm the diagram re-renders on next idle without duplicate DOM nodes.

automated: Expand `mermaidDecoration.test.ts` (or add `diagramDecoration.test.ts`) to cover the additional language tags and error isolation.
manual: Open the Typora diagram sample document and confirm parity rendering.

features.notes: Typora supports flowchart.js, js-sequence-diagrams, and Mermaid in fenced blocks. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Draw-Diagrams-With-Markdown/ and https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.0/.

features.title: Document tabs keep multiple markdown files open in a single window
steps: Open two markdown files and confirm each lives in its own tab with dirty-state indicators.

Use Typora-style document-switch shortcuts (`Ctrl+Tab` / `Ctrl+Shift+Tab` on Windows/Linux, `Cmd+`` / `Cmd+Shift+`` on macOS) and confirm the active tab changes while caret, selection, outline position, and undo history persist per tab.
Close a clean tab, trigger `Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+T`, and confirm the most recently closed document reopens in the same window with its prior tab state restored.
Close a dirty tab and confirm a save/discard/cancel prompt appears and respects the user choice.

automated: Add tab-state tests (open/switch/close/reopen/persist) in `App.test.tsx` and a desktop counterpart covering keyboard tab cycling, reopen-closed history, and window session restore.
manual: Open five tabs, cycle them with the platform shortcuts, reopen the last closed tab, then quit and relaunch to confirm session restore reproduces the tab set.

features.notes: Typora's later 1.x multi-tab workflow includes switching between opened documents and reopening the last closed file from the keyboard, not just showing multiple tabs. Repo search found no MarkFlow tab/session model, no keyboard tab-cycling bindings, and no reopen-closed stack in the current desktop/editor code, so the existing tab entry needed those acceptance criteria spelled out. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.5/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: UI localization supports switching interface language at runtime
steps: Expose a language selector in Preferences with at least English and one additional locale (e.g. 简体中文).
Switch the locale and confirm menus, toolbars, dialogs, and palette entries retranslate without requiring a restart.
Confirm document content is never translated and that per-user locale persists across launches.

automated: Add i18n resolver tests covering fallback chains and missing-key behavior.
manual: Switch between three locales and confirm full UI coverage plus right-to-left layout sanity if applicable.

features.notes: Typora release notes highlight expanding UI language coverage. Sources: https://support.typora.io/What%27s-New-1.13/ and https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Open Recent tracks recent files and folders, with clear-history and pinned-location behavior
steps: Open several files and at least one folder across multiple sessions and confirm `File` -> `Open Recent` lists them most-recent-first.
Pin a recent folder/location, clear recent items, and confirm pinned entries follow Typora's clear-options behavior while unpinned history is removed.
Click an entry whose file or folder is missing and confirm MarkFlow reports the error gracefully instead of crashing.

automated: Add desktop tests for recent-file and recent-folder persistence, menu serialization, pin/clear flows, and missing-path handling.
manual: Verify `Open Recent`, Quick Open, and OS-native recents all reflect the same saved history after restarting the app.

features.notes: Typora's File Management doc says recent files and folders are recorded in `File` -> `Open Recent`, Open Quickly, sidebar actions, and OS Dock/Taskbar integrations, with folder pinning plus `Clear Items` / `Clear Recent and Pinned Folders / Files` flows. MarkFlow currently only keeps an in-memory `recentFiles` array inside `packages/desktop/src/main/fileManager.ts` for `getQuickOpenList()`, with no persisted history, no `Open Recent` submenu, and no pin or clear behavior. Source: https://support.typora.io/File-Management/.

features.title: Always-on-top window toggle persists per-window across sessions
steps: Toggle Always on Top from the View menu and confirm the editor window stays above other apps.
Close and relaunch the app; confirm the previous window reopens with the same pinned state.
Confirm the setting is per-window when multiple windows are open.

automated: Add a desktop test stubbing `setAlwaysOnTop` and verifying state persistence read/write.
manual: Confirm behavior across macOS Spaces and Windows multi-monitor setups.

features.notes: Typora documents an Always on Top toggle. Source: https://support.typora.io/Menu/.

features.title: Back and forward navigation history jumps between visited headings and files
steps: Navigate through outline clicks, wikilinks, and global search results; confirm each destination is pushed onto history.
Press Cmd/Ctrl+[ (back) and Cmd/Ctrl+] (forward) and confirm the caret/viewport restores exactly to prior positions across documents.
Confirm history is capped at a reasonable depth (e.g. 64 entries) and old entries are trimmed.

automated: Add `navigationHistory.test.ts` covering push, back, forward, branching from mid-history, and cap enforcement.
manual: Traverse five headings across two files and confirm back/forward restores exact caret column plus scroll.

features.notes: Typora-style navigation benefits from a browser-like back/forward stack paralleling Obsidian/VS Code. Source: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Source mode displays a configurable line-number gutter
steps: Toggle source mode and confirm a left-side gutter shows 1-based line numbers aligned with content.
Expose a preference to hide the gutter and confirm toggling it off removes the column without reflowing decorations.
Confirm WYSIWYG mode never shows the gutter regardless of setting.

automated: Add an editor test asserting gutter presence/absence by mode and by preference toggle.
manual: Verify gutter alignment across 100+ line files and after folding sections.

features.notes: Typora exposes line numbers in source code mode under Preferences. Source: https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Insert date/time action writes the current timestamp in user-selected format
steps: Invoke Insert Date / Insert Time from the palette or shortcut.
Confirm the inserted string respects the format configured in Preferences (ISO 8601 default) and the user's locale/timezone.
Confirm the action respects current selection: replacing it rather than appending.

automated: Add an editor test covering date/time insertion with stubbed `Date.now()` and a custom format string.
manual: Insert timestamps under two different format preferences and confirm output matches.

features.notes: Typora includes configurable date/time insertion via shortcuts. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/ and https://support.typora.io/Preferences/.

features.title: Document statistics panel surfaces words, characters, reading time, and per-selection counts
steps: Open the statistics panel (menu or status-bar click) and confirm live totals for words, characters (with/without spaces), paragraphs, and approximate reading time.
Make a multi-block selection and confirm the panel adds a selection column alongside the document totals.
Confirm counts ignore fenced code content when the 'exclude code' preference is on.

automated: Extend `wordCount.test.ts` to cover paragraphs, reading-time approximation, and selection/exclusion modes.
manual: Cross-check counts against Typora on a shared sample document.

features.notes: Typora exposes an expanded word count / statistics popover. Source: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Custom keybinding editor lets users remap any palette-registered command
steps: Open the Shortcuts pane in Preferences and search for a command.
Record a new key chord and confirm it persists, conflicts are highlighted, and the prior binding is cleared.
Reset an individual binding and confirm the default returns without affecting other custom bindings.

automated: Add tests for binding storage, conflict detection, and reset semantics.
manual: Rebind three commands, relaunch, and confirm the new bindings fire.

features.notes: Typora allows customizing shortcuts via a JSON file and UI surface. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/ and https://support.typora.io/Custom-Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Embedded HTML media tags render preserved `<video>`, `<audio>`, and `<iframe>` content in WYSIWYG mode
steps: Insert raw HTML media tags such as `<video controls src='sample.mp4'></video>`, `<audio controls src='sample.mp3'></audio>`, and an embeddable `<iframe ... allowfullscreen></iframe>` snippet into a markdown document.

Render in WYSIWYG mode and confirm the media/embed element appears, required safe attributes (`src`, `controls`, `width`/`height`, `allowfullscreen`, etc.) survive, and the underlying markdown source is preserved.
Move the caret back into the HTML block and confirm the raw source becomes editable again without corrupting surrounding blocks or letting the embed navigate the editor shell.

manual: Render one local video, one local audio clip, and one remote iframe embed; confirm playback/loading works while the editor window itself never navigates away.

features.notes: Typora's current HTML and Media docs explicitly support `<video>`, `<audio>`, and `<iframe>` embeds, including drag-and-drop video insertion and sandboxed iframe/script handling. MarkFlow now preserves those tags in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/inlineHtmlDecoration.ts` with a small safe attribute allowlist, iframe sandboxing, and script/event-handler stripping, covered by `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/inlineHtmlDecoration.test.ts`. Manual local media/embed playback validation is still pending before this can move to `verified`. Sources: https://support.typora.io/HTML/ and https://support.typora.io/Media/.

features.title: HTML comments and HTML entities are preserved in WYSIWYG mode without leaking raw markup
steps: Insert HTML comments such as `<!-- hidden note -->` and HTML entities such as `©`, `®`, or ` ` into a markdown document.
Render in WYSIWYG mode and confirm comments do not surface as raw literal markup while entities display with their rendered character semantics.
Move the caret back into the affected ranges and confirm the underlying source remains editable without corrupting surrounding markdown.
features.notes: Typora documents support for HTML comments and HTML entities, and MarkFlow now preserves that behavior in `packages/editor/src/editor/decorations/inlineHtmlDecoration.ts` by extending the existing HTML replacement path to markdown `CommentBlock`, inline `Comment`, and `Entity` nodes. Focused regression coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/inlineHtmlDecoration.test.ts` verifies block comments stay hidden away from the caret, inline comments do not leak raw markup, entities render as decoded characters, and each range reveals its original source when the caret enters it. Verified on 2026-04-15 with `pnpm --filter @markflow/editor test:run -- src/editor/__tests__/inlineHtmlDecoration.test.ts` plus `node scripts/harness/verify.mjs`. Sources: https://support.typora.io/HTML/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Auto pair brackets, quotes, and markdown delimiters wrap selections and preserve typing context
steps: Type structural delimiters such as `(`, `[`, `{`, `"`, `'`, and backticks in the editor and confirm MarkFlow inserts the matching closer with the caret placed inside.

Select a word and type `(`, `*`, or `_`; confirm the selection is wrapped rather than replaced, and typing `*` at the start of a line still begins a list while `_` inside identifiers remains literal text.
Press Backspace inside an empty auto-inserted pair and confirm both sides are removed atomically without disturbing surrounding markdown.
features.notes: Verified on 2026-04-15. `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts` already covered structural pairs plus context-aware `*`/`_` pairing and atomic backspace; this run expanded `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts` to cover structural-pair selection wrapping and atomic deletion alongside the existing prose, unordered-list, and `snake_case` typing-context checks, so the listed automated commands fully prove the shipped scope. Typora's broader auto-pair parity for `~`, `=`, `$`, `^`, and a preference toggle remain separate gaps outside MF-099. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Auto-Pair/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Strict mode enforces GFM-style heading whitespace and list indentation rules
steps: Enable strict mode from a settings surface, restart if required, and type `###Header`; confirm it is not treated as a heading until a whitespace is added after the `#` characters.
Create ordered-list paragraphs and sublists with insufficient indentation and confirm strict mode follows GFM-style indentation instead of Typora's tolerant behavior.
Disable strict mode and confirm the same fixture returns to the more forgiving parsing behavior.

automated: Add parser/editor tests that run the same heading and list-indent fixtures under strict and tolerant modes.
Add settings-persistence coverage if the toggle is stored outside the editor state.
manual: Toggle strict mode on the same markdown fixture, restart, and verify heading/list parsing changes exactly as expected.

features.notes: Typora exposes a strict mode that follows GFM more closely, including mandatory whitespace after heading markers and stricter list-paragraph indentation, and applies the setting after restart. Repo search found no strict/tolerant parser toggle or persisted markdown-mode setting in MarkFlow yet. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Strict-Mode/ and https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/.

features.title: Delete range commands remove word, sentence or line, block, and styled scope with Typora-style semantics
steps: Trigger delete-word with the caret inside a word and confirm the current word is removed without corrupting adjacent markdown.
Trigger delete line or sentence inside prose, code blocks, math blocks, and tables and confirm the deletion respects the current block type instead of blindly deleting raw lines.
Trigger delete block or styled-scope on headings, list items, or inline styled spans and confirm only the intended markdown scope is removed.

automated: Add command-level tests for delete-word, delete-line-or-sentence, delete-block, and delete-styled-scope behavior across paragraph, list, code, math, and table fixtures.
manual: Exercise the delete-range shortcuts in a mixed markdown document and confirm each command removes the same scope Typora would remove.

features.notes: Typora documents block-aware delete commands for paragraph or block, sentence or line, styled scope, and word. MarkFlow currently relies on default CodeMirror deletion behavior, and repo search found no Typora-style delete-range command layer or shortcut wiring. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Delete-Range/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Zoom in, zoom out, and reset zoom adjust the window scale without disturbing document state
steps: Use View > Zoom In / Zoom Out or the matching shortcuts and confirm the window scale changes while the current document content, selection, and scroll position remain intact.
Trigger Actual Size / Reset Zoom and confirm the scale returns to baseline without recreating the editor view.
Continue editing after zooming and confirm typing, toolbar actions, and file save behavior still match the unzoomed editor.

automated: Add a desktop test that asserts the View menu exposes `resetZoom`, `zoomIn`, and `zoomOut` roles or equivalent BrowserWindow zoom commands.
manual: Interactive desktop check: zoom in, zoom out, and reset on a live document while watching selection and scroll stability.

features.notes: Typora exposes zoom from the View menu plus shortcut-driven reset or actual-size flows. MarkFlow already wires Electron `resetZoom`, `zoomIn`, and `zoomOut` roles in `packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts`, but the capability had no ledger entry, no explicit verification, and no Typora-like zoom state affordance yet. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Zoom/ and https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.

features.title: Launch behavior can reopen the last file/folder or open a configured default folder on startup
steps: Quit with a file or folder open and relaunch; confirm startup follows the selected launch option.
Switch to a default-folder launch option and confirm that folder opens on startup.
If the remembered/default path is missing, confirm MarkFlow falls back cleanly instead of crashing.

automated: Add desktop persistence tests for launch-option state and startup path selection.
manual: Verify clean relaunch behavior on macOS and Windows.

features.notes: Typora documents launch options for reopening the last file/folder or opening a specified folder on startup. MarkFlow currently only handles a CLI-passed markdown path and has no persisted startup preference or folder-restore flow. Sources: https://support.typora.io/File-Management/ and https://support.typora.io/Launch-Options/.

features.title: System dark mode applies separate light and dark themes automatically
steps: Choose different light and dark themes from the app theme controls and relaunch MarkFlow; confirm both theme mappings persist.
While MarkFlow is running, switch the OS appearance between light and dark and confirm the matching theme applies immediately without restart.
Return to each appearance mode and confirm the previously selected light and dark theme ids are still preserved.

automated: pnpm --filter @markflow/editor exec vitest run src/__tests__/App.test.tsx -t "manages separate light and dark themes and reflects runtime appearance switches"
manual: Open the desktop app, set distinct light and dark themes, switch the macOS or Windows appearance while the app stays open, and confirm the rendered theme and the active appearance indicator update immediately.

features.notes: Typora documents separate light and dark theme behavior that follows system appearance automatically. This run extends `packages/desktop/src/main/themeManager.ts`, `packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts`, `packages/shared/src/index.ts`, and `packages/editor/src/App.tsx` so MarkFlow persists independent light/dark theme ids, exposes them to the renderer, and updates the active theme on Electron `nativeTheme` changes; focused desktop and renderer tests were added, but manual OS-level verification is still pending so `passes` remains false. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Dark-Mode/ and https://support.typora.io/.

features.title: Enter creates a new paragraph while Shift+Enter inserts a single line break with Typora-style source mapping
steps: In WYSIWYG mode, place the caret at the end of a plain paragraph and press Enter; confirm the editor starts a new paragraph instead of inserting a lone source newline.
Switch to source mode after that edit and confirm the markdown now contains a blank line between the two paragraphs.
In WYSIWYG mode, press Shift+Enter inside a paragraph and confirm the editor inserts a single visible line break while keeping the text in the same paragraph when rendered.
features.notes: Typora's Markdown Reference and Line Break docs explicitly distinguish `Return` for a new paragraph from `Shift+Return` for a single line break, with source mode showing a blank line between paragraphs. The shipped editor behavior in `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts` is now locked by both keymap-level tests and real MarkFlowEditor DOM keydown coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts` and `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx`, including plain-paragraph Enter, Shift+Enter, source/split raw Enter behavior, and unordered/ordered/task list continuation plus empty-item exit so the paragraph-break feature cannot silently regress list handling. Automated verification passed on 2026-04-16 with the listed vitest, lint, build, and harness verification commands, so this feature can be truthfully marked verified without a separate desktop-only gate. Sources: https://support.typora.io/Markdown-Reference/ and https://support.typora.io/Line-Break/.

features.title: Paragraph shortcuts insert table, code fence, and math block scaffolds without raw markdown typing
steps: Place the caret on a plain paragraph and press `Ctrl+T` on Windows/Linux or `Cmd+Opt+T` on macOS; confirm MarkFlow inserts a valid markdown table scaffold with a header row, separator row, and one body row, then places the caret inside the first editable cell.
Press `Ctrl+Shift+K` on Windows/Linux or `Cmd+Opt+C` on macOS; confirm a fenced code block scaffold is inserted at the current block and the caret lands inside the fence body.

Press `Ctrl+Shift+M` on Windows/Linux or `Cmd+Opt+B` on macOS; confirm a `$$` math block scaffold is inserted at the current block and the caret lands inside the math body without corrupting surrounding markdown.
features.notes: Updated on 2026-04-16: `packages/editor/src/editor/extensions/smartInput.ts` inserts table, fenced code block, and math block markdown scaffolds only when the active selection is a plain paragraph in WYSIWYG mode, using Typora-aligned bindings (`Ctrl+T`, `Ctrl+Shift+K`, `Ctrl+Shift+M` on Windows/Linux; `Cmd+Opt+T`, `Cmd+Opt+C`, `Cmd+Opt+B` on macOS). Automated coverage in `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.test.ts`, `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/smartInput.mac.test.ts`, and `packages/editor/src/editor/__tests__/MarkFlowEditor.test.tsx` now proves the Win/Linux DOM shortcut path, a simulated Mac DOM shortcut path under `navigator.platform = MacIntel`, scaffold text/caret placement, source-mode editability after toggle, and no-op behavior outside WYSIWYG/non-paragraph contexts, so the feature can be truthfully marked verified. Source: https://support.typora.io/Shortcut-Keys/.
